title: Fail incomplete installs and align release documentation
outOfScope: Adding real-project CI for every Preset-only stack
Claiming every lifecycle-critical module reaches 70 percent coverage in this Work Item

sources.reason: Reports partial-install success, stale multilingual release claims, and critical orchestration coverage gaps.
Owns conflict behavior, transaction rollback, and installed runtime validation.
Defines the published adoption and SHA256 capability source of truth.

riskAssessment.reason: Changing first-install ownership and post-install validation affects every target repository.

executionDecision.reason: Conflict detection, import validation, Make parsing, and capability text are reproducible in isolated tests.

preReviewWarnings: Project-owned glossary and customized stack commands must remain preservable.
Post-install validation failures must roll back first installs as well as upgrades.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A fresh install fails before writing when a managed target file exists with different content and reports every conflict path.
Identical managed files remain idempotent and project-owned glossary behavior remains unchanged.
Every non-dry-run install validates managed file contents, imports all installed Python runtime modules, and parses the installed Make entrypoint.
A post-install validation failure rolls back a first install to its original tree.
All three READMEs state that the current public release includes auditable first adoption.
Documentation metadata tests prevent multilingual adoption capability drift.
Direct tests expand critical finish/checkpoint failure and helper coverage where bounded.
All required governance and project checks pass.
guidelines: Never report installation success when managed runtime files are incomplete or inconsistent.
Detect conflicts before the first write and list actionable repository-relative paths.
Preserve explicitly project-owned files and idempotent identical content.
Published capability statements must derive from release metadata and remain synchronized across languages.
Do not hand-edit generated cockpit status.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The requested fix modifies installer trust boundaries and governance documentation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
